headline: Passport Backlog Reaches 500,000 Amid Emigration Surge
context: The Immigration Department reported a backlog of 500,000 passport applications in March 2026, driven by a 48% increase in applications as Malaysians — particularly young professionals — seek overseas employment and residency.

cards.big: 500,000 passport applications backlogged — 48% surge reflects an exodus of talent seeking opportunities abroad
cards.sub: 1.86 million Malaysians now live overseas permanently — up from 1.5 million in 2020

cards.big: Brain drain costs Malaysia estimated RM48 billion annually in lost human capital and tax revenue
cards.sub: 78% of overseas Malaysians hold university degrees — the country exports its most educated citizens

cards.big: Singapore employs 420,000 Malaysian professionals — average salary 2.8x higher than equivalent Malaysian positions
cards.sub: Monthly JB-Singapore commuters number 350,000 — earning Singapore wages while Malaysian talent market empties

cards.big: Immigration Department operates with 1,200 passport processing staff — unchanged since 2018 despite 48% demand increase
cards.sub: Online appointment system crashes regularly with 280,000 users competing for 8,000 daily processing slots

cards.big: The passport backlog is just the visible symptom — the real crisis is why so many Malaysians want to leave
Should Malaysia survey every passport applicant on emigration intent and address the top 5 reasons people are leaving?
